TRANSLATION:
```python
def parseRules(scanner, location: str, ruleMapping: Dict[str, List['Rule']], asciiFoldings: Dict[str, str]):
    currentLine = 0
    inMultilineComment = False

    while scanner.hasNextLine():
        currentLine += 1
        rawLine = scanner.nextLine()
        line = rawLine

        if inMultilineComment:
            if line.endswith(MULTILINE_COMMENT_END):
                inMultilineComment = False
            continue

        if line.startswith(MULTILINE_COMMENT_START):
            inMultilineComment = True
        else:
            cmtI = line.find(COMMENT)
            if cmtI >= 0:
                line = line[:cmtI]

            line = line.strip()

            if not line:
                continue   # empty lines can be safely skipped

            if '=' in line:
                parts = line.split('=')
                if len(parts) != 2:
                    raise ValueError(f"Malformed folding statement split into {len(parts)} parts: {rawLine} in {location}")
                
                leftCharacter, rightCharacter = parts[0], parts[1]

                if len(leftCharacter) != 1 or len(rightCharacter) != 1:
                    raise ValueError(f"Malformed folding statement - patterns are not single characters: {rawLine} in {location}")
                
                asciiFoldings[leftCharacter] = rightCharacter
            else:
                parts = line.split()
                if len(parts) != 4:
                    raise ValueError(f"Malformed rule statement split into {len(parts)} parts: {rawLine} in {location}")
                
                pattern, replacement1, replacement2, replacement3 = map(stripQuotes, parts)

                r = Rule(pattern, replacement1, replacement2, replacement3)
                patternKey = r.pattern[0]
                rules = ruleMapping.get(patternKey, [])
                rules.append(r)
                ruleMapping[patternKey] = rules
```
